trivia_bullets.text: Haunted Screen is an uncommon artifact from Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK), released September 27, 2024. Televisions, VHS tapes, and CRT screens appear throughout the set as horror-delivery vectors in keeping with the retro consumer-electronics dread theme.

Haunted Screen is a five-color mana rock designed as a visual joke: it produces white or black mana for free (like a black-and-white TV), but requires paying 1 life to add green, blue, or red (like paying to upgrade to color). Surfaced by the EDHREC Duskmourn artifacts review.
sources: EDHREC: Duskmourn Set Review -- Artifacts and Lands

trivia_bullets.text: Because Haunted Screen's oracle text contains all five mana symbols, its Commander color identity is WUBRG, restricting it to five-color decks only. It cannot appear in two-color Commander decks despite being a colorless artifact. Competes with Timeless Lotus and Mox Amber in five-color mana-rock slots.
sources: EDHREC: Duskmourn Set Review -- Artifacts and Lands

trivia_bullets.text: The {7} activation puts seven +1/+1 counters on Haunted Screen and makes it a 0/0 Spirit creature in addition to its other types (once only), yielding a 7/7 Spirit. EDHREC describes this as a secondary lategame payoff for five-color brewers.
sources: EDHREC: Duskmourn Set Review -- Artifacts and Lands

related_cards.name: Timeless Lotus
related_cards.note: five-color mana rock Haunted Screen competes with in Commander

related_cards.name: Coveted Jewel
related_cards.note: another high-cost artifact with a lategame payoff

triage_flags.sweep_note: tags_filter has 'colorless' (not 'colorless-magic') -- functionally equivalent but corpus convention should be verified. Low severity.
